creator: pen@lysator.liu.se
text:
After updating the firmware on the Intel X710 cards to the latest from Dell (Network_Firmware_M20T0_WN64_20.5.13_A00.EXE) on a Dell PowerEdge R740xd with FreeBSD 12.2-RELEASE-p10, we've started seeing these errors at bootup. Things seems to work fine though, traffic flows:

% dmesg|egrep ixl0
ixl0: <Intel(R) Ethernet Controller X710 for 10GbE SFP+ - 2.3.0-k> mem 0xae000000-0xaeffffff,0xaf018000-0xaf01ffff at device 0.0 numa-domain 0 on pci7
ixl0: fw 8.84.66032 api 1.14 nvm 8.40 etid 8000af84 oem 20.5120.13
ixl0: The driver for the device detected a newer version of the NVM image than expected.
ixl0: Please install the most recent version of the network driver.
ixl0: PF-ID[0]: VFs 32, MSI-X 129, VF MSI-X 5, QPs 384, I2C
ixl0: Using 1024 TX descriptors and 1024 RX descriptors
ixl0: Using 12 RX queues 12 TX queues
ixl0: Using MSI-X interrupts with 13 vectors
ixl0: Ethernet address: 3c:fd:fe:2f:36:a0
ixl0: Allocating 16 queues for PF LAN VSI; 12 queues active
ixl0: PCI Express Bus: Speed 8.0GT/s Width x8
ixl0: Failed to initialize SR-IOV (error=2)
ixl0: netmap queues/slots: TX 12/1024, RX 12/1024
ixl0: Link is up, 10 Gbps Full Duplex, Requested FEC: None, Negotiated FEC: None, Autoneg: False, Flow Control: None
ixl0: link state changed to UP


% egrep 'ixl0|lagg' /etc/rc.conf
cloned_interfaces="lagg0"
ifconfig_lagg0="laggproto failover laggport ixl0 laggport ixl2 130.236.8.38 netmask 255.255.255.224 -use_flowid"
ifconfig_lagg0_ipv6="inet6 2001:6b0:17:2400::8:38/64 -use_flowid"
ifconfig_ixl0="up"

% ifconfig ixl0
ixl0: flags=8843<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,SIMPLEX,MULTICAST> metric 0 mtu 1500
	options=e507bb<RXCSUM,TXCSUM,VLAN_MTU,VLAN_HWTAGGING,JUMBO_MTU,VLAN_HWCSUM,TSO4,TSO6,LRO,VLAN_HWFILTER,VLAN_HWTSO,RXCSUM_IPV6,TXCSUM_IPV6>
	ether 3c:fd:fe:2f:36:a0
	media: Ethernet autoselect (10Gbase-Twinax <full-duplex>)
	status: active
	nd6 options=29<PERFORMNUD,IFDISABLED,AUTO_LINKLOCAL>
